system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

);

    }

            (1 2 6 5)
        );

);

mergePatchPairs

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-06;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-04;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phid,p)     Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(meshPhi,p)  Gauss upwind;
    div((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U)))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

odeCoeffs

    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword specie is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties/CH4"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties/CH4 from line 34 to line 36.

    From function const Foam::dictionary& Foam::dictionary::subDict(const Foam::word&) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 1008.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
keyword specie is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties/CH4"
```

This occurs because OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture` is looking for a `specie` sub-dictionary inside the `CH4` file (which is the species thermodynamic properties file in `constant/`). The solver expects each species file in `constant/` to contain a `specie` keyword, but the current `CH4` file in `constant/` does not have one.

Looking at the history of attempts, various approaches have been tried regarding the `specie` keyword placement. The fundamental issue is that in OpenFOAM 10 with `hePsiThermo` and `multiComponentMixture`, the framework expects each species' thermodynamic dictionary (in `constant/`) to contain a `specie` entry that maps the species name to its thermodynamic data.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Add `specie` entry to each species file in `constant/`

Each file in `constant/` that corresponds to a species (i.e., `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, `CO2`) needs to contain a `specie` sub-dictionary. For each species file, add:

```
specie
{
    name        "<SpeciesName>";
}
```

For example, in `constant/CH4`:

```
specie
{
    name        "CH4";
}
```

Repeat this for `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2` files in `constant/`. This tells OpenFOAM 10's thermophysical model how to identify each species.

### 2. Clean up `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Ensure `thermo.compressibleGas` also has matching `specie` entries for each species. Each species entry should look like:

```
CH4
{
    specie
    {
        name        "CH4";
    }
    h           8.893e5;
    Cp          4.662e1;
    Cv          3.930e1;
}
```

...and similarly for the other species.

### 3. Remove conflicting `specie` entries from `physicalProperties`

If `physicalProperties` has a `specie` dictionary mapping (e.g., `{ CH4 CH4; O2 O2; ... }`), it should remain. But if it has standalone `specie specie;` entries, those should be removed since they refer to a non-existent sub-dictionary.

### Summary

The key fix is ensuring every species thermodynamic file in `constant/` (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2) contains a `specie { name "SpeciesName"; }` sub-dictionary. This is required by OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture`/`hePsiThermo` combination to properly resolve species identities. Previous attempts focused on `physicalProperties` having the wrong `specie` format — this shifts the focus to the individual species files in `constant/`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.2 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
